SEARCH
Package cpdt.simulator

Class SensorDevice

java.lang.Object
cpdt.common.models.Device
cpdt.simulator.SensorDevice
Direct Known Subclasses:
FlowSensor, GasSensor, LevelSensor, PhSensor, PressureSensor, TemperatureSensor
public abstract class SensorDevice extends Device
Base abstraction for all field sensors.

Provides the common properties and behavior shared by all sensor implementations, including ADC quantization, hardware resolution calculation, long-term drift simulation, and access to the simulated plant environment. Concrete subclasses extend this functionality by implementing device-specific measurement models and sensor physics.

This class follows the Template Method Design pattern by providing shared functionality while delegating sensor-specific measurement logic to subclasses.

Since:
1.0
Author:
Lakshmi Sanjeev

  • Constructor Details

    • SensorDevice

      protected SensorDevice(String deviceId, String name, DeviceType deviceType, Location location, MeasurementType measurementType, PlantEnvironment plantEnvironment)
      Creates a new sensor device. The parameters deviceId, name, type, and location have the same meaning as defined by Device(String, String, DeviceType, Location).
      Parameters:
      measurementType - physical quantity measured by the sensor
      plantEnvironment - simulated plant environment from which the sensor obtains measurement values

  • Method Details

    • getReading

      public abstract double getReading()
      Obtains the current sensor reading.

      Concrete sensor implementations must override this method to apply their device-specific measurement model, including any filtering, compensation, noise, drift, and hardware effects.

      Returns:
      the measured value in the engineering units defined by the associated MeasurementType

    • getEnvironmentValue

      protected double getEnvironmentValue()
      Retrieves the current environmental value corresponding to this sensor's measurement type and process area.

      This method provides the underlying physical value that sensor implementations observe before applying device-specific behavior.

      Returns:
      the current environmental value for this sensor

    • applyAdcQuantization

      protected double applyAdcQuantization(double value)
      Applies 16-bit ADC quantization to a measured value.

      The supplied value is normalized to the sensor's operating range, quantized to the nearest representable ADC level, and converted back to engineering units to simulate the behavior of a real analog-to- digital converter.

      Parameters:
      value - measured value before quantization
      Returns:
      the quantized measurement

    • updateLongTermDrift

      protected void updateLongTermDrift(double deltaTimeSeconds, ThreadLocalRandom random)
      Updates the accumulated long-term sensor drift.

      Drift is modeled as a Wiener process (Brownian motion), where the drift increment for each sampling interval is drawn from a Gaussian distribution scaled according to the elapsed time and configured drift variance.

      Parameters:
      deltaTimeSeconds - elapsed time since the previous update, in seconds
      random - thread-local random number generator used to sample the drift increment

    • calculateHardwareResolution

      protected void calculateHardwareResolution()
      Calculates the hardware resolution of the sensor.

      The resolution is determined from the configured measurement range and the simulated ADC resolution, representing the smallest measurable change that can be distinguished by the sensor.
